seo.title: AKUU, international solidarity in the Peruvian Amazon
seo.description: AKUU works for sustainable, solidarity-based development that respects Amazonian communities in Puerto Miguel, Peru.

hero.subtitle: In the Amazon and in France, AKUU connects volunteers and local communities around concrete solidarity projects.
hero.image_alt: Peruvian Amazon, tropical forest and river

mission.title: Our mission
mission.text: AKUU works for sustainable, solidarity-based development that respects Amazonian populations, placing local cooperation, knowledge sharing and environmental protection at the heart of its actions.
mission.image_alt: Puerto Miguel village in the Amazon

featured_project.title: Featured project
featured_project.subtitle: Shapishiko Museum
featured_project.description: A cultural space in the heart of the Amazon to preserve and promote Kukama culture.
featured_project.image_alt: Shapishiko Museum, cultural project in Puerto Miguel

donation.title: Support our actions
donation.text: Every donation directly contributes to the sustainable development of Puerto Miguel village and the preservation of Kukama culture.

social.title: Follow us
social.subtitle: Follow our adventures and projects in real time on social media

partners.title: Our partners
partners.subtitle: They trust us and support our projects

association.who_text: AKUU is a French non-profit (loi 1901), founded on November 23, 2016. It carries out field missions in the Peruvian Amazon, mainly in Puerto Miguel, and runs awareness campaigns in the home countries of its volunteers.
association.quote: AKUU is a project I believe in with all my strength. AKUU reminds me of my connection with nature, with traditions. This word means the sharing of energies, the respect for the forest, its fauna, its flora...
association.itv_placeholder: Report video coming soon. In the meantime, see the “Voices from the Amazon” interviews below.

durable.title: Sustainable development
durable.text: Promoting solutions that respect the environment and local resources.

solidarite.title: International solidarity
solidarite.text: Building bridges between cultures and fostering mutual aid between peoples.

culture.title: Respect for local cultures
culture.text: Valuing and preserving the ancestral knowledge of Kukama communities.

savoirs.title: Knowledge sharing
savoirs.text: Sharing knowledge to strengthen community autonomy.

environnement.title: Environmental protection
environnement.text: Taking concrete action to preserve the Amazon rainforest.

echanges.title: Intercultural exchanges
echanges.text: Fostering mutual understanding through sharing and meeting.

association.odd_subtitle: AKUU's actions are directly aligned with the United Nations 2030 Agenda. Each of our projects contributes to at least one SDG.
association.social_subtitle: Follow our news, photos and videos from the field and the life of the association.
association.social_subtitle_tiktok_only: Videos and posts from the field and the life of the association.
association.social_fallback_instagram: Updates, reels and behind the scenes on {'@'}akuu_association.

legal.siege: Headquarters in France. Postal address available on request via the contact form.

2016.title: Founding of the association
2016.description: AKUU is founded on 23 November 2016 to promote sustainable management of Amazonian resources.

parts.text: AKUU is officially founded on 23 November 2016, with a mission to foster sustainable management of Amazonian resources through solidarity projects and awareness campaigns.

parts.title: Founding partners
parts.text: INP Foundation, Engagé.e.s et Déterminé.e.s, WindAid Institute.

2017.title: First steps
2017.description: First field missions in Puerto Miguel and first awareness actions in France.

parts.title: Arrival in Puerto Miguel
parts.text: First field missions in Puerto Miguel. Start of impact assessments, meetings with communities, and groundbreaking for Casa AKUU, the association’s future hub.

parts.title: First awareness events
parts.text: First awareness actions in France on climate issues and Amazon conservation.

2018.title: Growth
2018.description: Casa AKUU opens, English classes, Hydr'Ama, energy, water, and outreach in France.

parts.title: Opening of Casa AKUU
parts.text: Opening of the community house in Puerto Miguel, a shared living and working space for volunteers and residents.

parts.title: English classes
parts.text: Launch of the English programme in Puerto Miguel, at the request of the village school head. Three groups: niños, secundarios, adultos.

parts.title: Hydr'Ama project
parts.text: Design and build of a hydrokinetic turbine with WindAid Institute. Deployment in Vista Alegre with strong community involvement in the floating platform. Approval by Loreto regional authorities.

parts.title: Solar power
parts.text: Solar panels installed in Puerto Miguel and Vista Alegre to power the community house, the art school, and shared spaces.

parts.title: Drinking water
parts.text: Rainwater harvesting and filtration system installed in Vista Alegre.

parts.title: Awareness in France
parts.text: Climax festival in Bordeaux, student events (INP Foundation, Phelma), signing of the appeal for an ecological constitution.

parts.title: New partners
parts.text: ASSHUME, Foundation for Nature and Humankind, FONJEP, RESES.

2019.title: Innovation
2019.description: AKUU'Be, Hydr'Ama follow-up, Solid'AKUU, Bagazan, AKUUVision, Humanos, outreach.

parts.title: AKUU'Be project
parts.text: Plastic waste management mission in Puerto Miguel: building recycling machines (shredder, injection), collection loop, agreement with Nauta municipality for waste treatment, a first in the Amazon.

parts.title: Hydr'Ama: follow-up mission
parts.text: Field mission in Vista Alegre to test the turbine in real conditions and plan upgrades. Identification of other potential villages.

parts.title: Solid'AKUU
parts.text: Continued drinking-water projects with student association Solida'rire.

parts.title: Bagazan project
parts.text: Impact assessment mission in Bagazan village (Ucayali River) ahead of future projects.

parts.title: AKUUVision
parts.text: Augmented reality project for climate awareness. Winner of the national PIEED competition in Paris.

parts.title: Humanos de Puerto Miguel
parts.text: Portraits and testimonies from Puerto Miguel residents, shared on social media to show life in the Amazon.

parts.title: Awareness
parts.text: Imaginarium Festival at UTC Compiègne, Climate Fresk training, talks in secondary schools and universities, participation in an awareness event in Iquitos, Peru.

parts.title: New partners
parts.text: IDEX, Université Grenoble Alpes (UGA).

2020.title: COVID halt
2020.description: The pandemic stops field missions; the team stays connected remotely.

parts.text: The global pandemic halts all field missions. The team keeps in touch remotely with Puerto Miguel and begins strategic thinking on the association’s future.

2021.title: Restructuring
2021.description: Governance overhaul and priorities refocused on one long-term flagship project.

parts.title: Internal reset
parts.text: Restructuring how the association works. Simpler governance, renewed teams, and priorities reshaped around one central long-term project.

2023.title: New chapter
2023.description: Shapishiko Museum project, renovation in Puerto Miguel, FONJEP partnership.

parts.title: Birth of the Shapishiko Museum project
parts.text: A museum project had first been imagined in 2019 with the Las Mariposas artisans; taken up again in 2023, it moves forward with preliminary studies and a dossier for a museum dedicated to Kukama culture in Puerto Miguel. First field missions after COVID.

parts.title: Renovation and missions
parts.text: Start of building renovation in Puerto Miguel. Several volunteer missions relaunch activity on the ground.

parts.title: Partner
parts.text: FONJEP (renewal).

2024.title: Construction
2024.description: Regular missions in Puerto Miguel: Casa AKUU, museum, community ties.

parts.title: Field missions
parts.text: Regular missions in Puerto Miguel: Casa AKUU renovation, progress on the Shapishiko Museum, stronger links with the local community and Kukama artisans.

2025.title: Acceleration
2025.description: Main structure of the cultural interpretation centre, Casa renovated, museum production, civic service, outreach, new partners.

parts.title: Main structure of the cultural interpretation centre
parts.text: Construction of the full main structure of the cultural interpretation centre (Shapishiko Museum project) in Puerto Miguel, shell and building envelope.

parts.title: Casa AKUU renovated
parts.text: Major upgrades: sanitation, kitchen, solar electricity, bedrooms. Casa AKUU becomes a real year-round home for volunteers.

parts.title: Shapishiko Museum: producing works
parts.text: First museum pieces produced with local artists: paintings, sculptures, chambira fibre creations. Grant from the Peruvian Ministry of Culture to support artistic production.

parts.title: Civic service
parts.text: First civic service volunteers on long missions in Puerto Miguel through the partnership with AIME ONG and France Volontaires.

parts.title: Awareness
parts.text: Continued outreach in France: social media, newsletter, school visits.

parts.title: New partners
parts.text: AIME ONG, France Volontaires, ICAM, PCM-Cultura.

2026.title: On the horizon
2026.description: Interior finishing for the interpretation centre, museum opening, Peruvian NGO registration, partner ONG Faver.

parts.title: Interior finishing of the cultural interpretation centre
parts.text: Interior fitting, scenography, finishes and equipment to prepare for welcoming the public.

parts.title: Opening of the Shapishiko Museum
parts.text: Planned opening of the Shapishiko Museum in Puerto Miguel: a living space for Kukama culture and knowledge, open to residents, travellers, and researchers.

parts.title: Official registration in Peru
parts.text: AKUU plans to reopen its Peruvian association, closed since COVID, to strengthen its local institutional footing.

parts.title: Partner
parts.text: ONG Faver.

projets.hero_subtitle: Concrete actions for a sustainable future in the Amazon

musee-shapishiko.title: Shapishiko Museum
musee-shapishiko.description: Building a cultural space in Puerto Miguel to preserve and promote Kukama culture.

casa-akuu.title: Casa AKUU
casa-akuu.description: Community house to host volunteers and run the association's activities.

cours-anglais.title: English classes
cours-anglais.description: Community-led programme (children, teens, adults) for local ecotourism. Suspended since COVID; relaunch in preparation.

hydrama.title: HYDR'AMA, river energy
hydrama.description: River turbine with WindAid Institute in Vista Alegre: clean power without a dam, open-source approach, communities at the centre. Suspended since COVID; relaunch in preparation.

sensibilisation-climat.title: AKUUVision
sensibilisation-climat.description: Raising awareness on climate and Amazonian issues through AKUUVision: Climate Fresk workshops, Planet'AKUU, Forever the Game, in France and Puerto Miguel.

musee.hero_subtitle: Kukama culture, cosmovision and territory, in Puerto Miguel, where the Ucayali and Marañón meet to form the Amazon.
musee.description_intro: The Shapishiko Museum grew from the Las Mariposas artisans' initiative, with AKUU's support and the Puerto Miguel community. At the heart of Kukama territory on the Ucayali, this living space aims to uplift Indigenous culture while welcoming villagers and visitors.
description_objectives: Keep Kukama culture alive and visible
Raise awareness of Amazon forest conservation
Redirect part of existing tourism toward the village and strengthen local income
Create a lively place for exchange and transmission
musee.description_committee: A village management committee, 17 members, women and men aged 21 to 82, co-steers the project with AKUU. Progress is shared regularly at village assemblies.
musee.context_p1: Climate change and deforestation threaten the Amazon; protecting and valuing Indigenous territories helps address that. Our approach links culture, environment and the local economy.
musee.context_p2: Puerto Miguel has about 400 residents, mostly descendants of the Kukama-Kukamiria peoples. The language and ancestral knowledge are fragile; yet local initiatives, Las Mariposas, Radio Ucamara, village artists, show strong resilience and creativity.
musee.context_p3: The village sits in a tourist area (lodges on the Ucayali) where benefits rarely reach the community. The museum aims to become a draw within Puerto Miguel so value and attention flow back to residents.

mariposas.title: Las Mariposas
mariposas.text: Village artisans' association that sparked the project: chambira value chain and certification. They help create the museum's artworks alongside village artists.

nelvis.title: Nelvis Paredes Pacaya
nelvis.text: Painter from the village; he teaches painting to local young people, who take part in creating the museum's artworks. He also volunteers to pass on Shipibo and Kukama cultures to preserve knowledge.

ecole_art.title: Puerto Miguel art school
ecole_art.text: Director: Casilda Pinches Sanchez. With extensive experience teaching art to the village’s children, she makes the studio a place of sharing knowledge and creation. Her commitment remains rooted in ties to the museum project and local artists.
ecole_art.photo_alt: Puerto Miguel art school, Casilda Pinches Sanchez

ucamara.title: Radio Ucamara
ucamara.text: Territory and river advocacy, promotion of Kukama culture, research and education; a key partner for visibility and content.

chullachaqui.title: Chullachaqui Ecomuseum
chullachaqui.text: Near Iquitos; exchange on Amazonian cultures, training local guides and cooperation on museum content.

akuu.title: AKUU
akuu.text: French nonprofit supporting the project from the impact study, mobilising volunteers and funding, and gradually stepping back in favour of village governance.

musee.coupe_path_lead: The route runs from the interpretation centre on three levels (water, dense forest, canopy) to the cultural space via the Tsakarita walkway (Ikuachiru), then the shop and map of village artisans. The interactive section below shows each space in detail; scroll to explore and discover their Kukama names.
musee.governance_body:
The museum shop complements local offerings; part of the income helps maintain the site. Las Mariposas contribute to creating artworks rather than running that shop. AKUU aims to support rather than decide on behalf of residents.

The project also seeks recognition within Peru's museum network (process underway with iPeru and relevant institutions).
musee.teams_body: Successive AKUU volunteer teams work on site; civil works and local supervision are coordinated with the village, notably by Abraham Fababa, an experienced construction lead from Puerto Miguel, with a team of about eleven skilled workers.
musee.visit_intro: Final arrangements will be set with the committee and local authorities.
musee.visit_duration: About thirty minutes per main interpretation room is planned; the full route (interpretation, cultural space, shop) may take around two hours depending on the group.
musee.visit_rates: Indicative pricing inspired by regional models: about 40 soles (roughly ten euros) for foreign visitors, free or preferential rates for local residents, subject to confirmation on site.
musee.visit_disclaimer: Non-binding information; please check with the museum before you travel.
musee.visit_photo_alt: Puerto Miguel village on the Ucayali River
musee.plans_intro: Site plan, architectural perspectives and the interactive ArchiCAD model. The section view of the spaces appears higher on this page. Drawings were developed by architect Maria Agustina Pistolesi, drawing inspiration notably from the Tree House Lodge in Puerto Miguel.
musee.gallery_subtitle: The Shapishiko Museum build, from design to work on the ground.

context.alt: Amazon forest and land around Puerto Miguel

governance.alt: Community life and decisions in the village

teams.alt: Build site and teams on the ground

musee.support_text: Contribute to the construction of the Shapishiko Museum and help us preserve Kukama culture for future generations.

coupe.title: Museum spaces
coupe.intro: The section drawing stays on screen while you scroll: each level is introduced in sequence. You can also tap a zone on the plan or the numbered buttons.
coupe.scroll_hint: Keep scrolling to explore all five spaces
coupe.scroll_track_aria: Progress through the section drawing tour
coupe.scroll_track_zone: Space {current} of {total}, keep scrolling for the next
coupe.scroll_track_outro: End of the drawing tour, more of the page below
coupe.alt: Architectural section of the Shapishiko Museum

kuruara.etage: Level 1, guardian of the second world
kuruara.description: The Kuruara is a spirit being who rules the submerged second world in Kukama cosmology. It rises from the depths, beautiful pink dolphin, shifting lights, water sounds. An outstretched hand on the ceiling wraps you in the heart of the floodable forest.

iwrati.etage: Level 2, guardian of the dense forest
iwrati.description: The Iw Rati Tuwa is a mischievous spirit roaming the forest depths, luring travellers only to lead them astray. Follow its trail and slip into the skin of the jaguar, the tapir and other dwellers of the dense forest.

tsumi.description: The Tsumi is the most powerful of Kukama healers, a spiritual traveller who can dive under water, rise into the air and take the shape of any animal. From the room at the top of the museum, look out over the forest canopy and follow the journeys of these sages whose art is fading today.

tsakarita.description: In Kukama, tsakarita means a shortcut, a direct link between two points. This walkway draws a straight line through time, plunging Kukama culture back into its pre-Columbian roots before reaching the exhibition space.

ikuachiru.description: In Kukama, ikuachiru is the place where knowledge lives. The last stop on the visit, both gallery and workshop: its walls tell stories, its tables host creation. Kukama art and craft are lived and passed on. A shop offers work by local artisans.

impact_terrain.label: Consultation and impact study (2019-2023)
items: Idea led by Las Mariposas; survey of Puerto Miguel residents
COVID pause and lessons; stronger partnerships
Partners: Radio Ucamara, Chullachaqui Ecomuseum

financement.label: 2024: Casa Redonda build, funding and renovation
items: Construction of the Casa Redonda cultural interpretation centre (main site work shown in the photo gallery)
FONJEP/JSI funding secured
Building renovation

subvention_casa.label: 2025: Casa Redonda continued, grant and Casa AKUU
items: Further work on Casa Redonda and the interpretation centre structure
Village management committee formed (17 members, ages 21-82)
Grant from the Peruvian Ministry of Culture
Casa AKUU improved
Artistic design of the interpretation centre

production.label: 2026: interior finishing, works and training
items: Ministry of Culture (support for artistic content)
FAVER (project support)
Purchase and production of works (wave 1, April to June 2026)
Works and interior finishing, first floor of the interpretation centre, cultural space, sanitation (April to July 2026)
Initial training for the management committee (May to September 2026)
Cultural programme and first public workshops (from September 2026)
Completion of other levels, Tsakarita walkway, exterior (October 2026 to January 2027)
Purchases and materials, wave 2 (November 2026 to March 2027)

ouverture.label: Opening and consolidation (2026 to 2027)
items: Partial opening planned for September 2026
Further committee training (January to May 2027)
Full official opening planned for June 2027
Cultural programme and evaluation period (June to December 2027)

volontaires.hero_subtitle: Live a unique experience in the heart of the Peruvian Amazon

immersion.title: Real responsibilities
immersion.text: You respond to a need voiced directly by the community and carry your mission through from start to finish.

impact.title: Concrete action for the Amazon
impact.text: People who live in the forest are its first protectors. Working with them is the most direct way to contribute.

skills.title: Learn as much as you teach
skills.text: Amazonian communities hold knowledge and a worldview that change those who come to meet them.

community.title: An experience that counts
community.text: Within a long-term project, alongside people who have chosen to welcome you.

step1.title: Application
step1.text: Send your application via our contact form.

step2.title: Interview
step2.text: Discussion with the team to validate your motivation.

step4.title: Mission
step4.text: Departure for Puerto Miguel and immersion in projects.

civique.title: Civic service
civique.text: Via AIME ONG / France Volontaires. 6 to 12 months.

lea.quote: My time in Puerto Miguel changed how I see the world. Living alongside Kukama communities taught me what matters: sharing and respect for nature.

dylan.quote: Helping build the museum was the most rewarding experience of my life. You feel you're contributing to something lasting.

raphael.quote: Teaching English to children in Puerto Miguel opens a window on the world for them, and I learned so much from them in return.

volontaires.cta_text: Contact us to learn more about available missions.

soutien.label: Take action
soutien.hero_text1: All of AKUU's running costs (banking, logistics, website) are covered by our volunteers and close supporters.
soutien.simulator_note: 66% tax deduction for French residents (art. 200 CGI)
soutien.widget_badge: Monthly donation recommended · Cancel anytime · Automatic tax receipt
soutien.widget_secure: Secure payment by HelloAsso · Zero fees on your donation
soutien.other_subtitle: Beyond donating, join the AKUU adventure
soutien.adherer_text: From €10/year, become a member, attend general assemblies and receive our activity reports.
soutien.benevole_text: 3 to 6 months in Puerto Miguel or in France for AKUUVision. Construction, education, culture: a unique human experience.
soutien.partenaire_text: Company, institution, university: join us for a lasting and visible impact.

hydrama.hero_subtitle: River energy at the service of Amazonian communities
hydrama.suspended_notice: On hold since COVID (funding). The sections below expand the story and what it means; the timeline then summarizes the key dates.

utc_2017.label: 2017 · UTC Compiègne
items: Bespoke design, turbine tailored to river constraints: low current, seasonal variation, logistical isolation.

trujillo.label: Feb. to Jun. 2018 · WindAid workshops, Trujillo
items: Manufacturing in the workshop alongside WindAid technicians. Parts, rotor, basin tests. Locally available materials, fully manual assembly.

turbine_ready.label: Jun. 2018 · Prototype delivered
items: First AKUU and WindAid hydrokinetic turbine completed.

vista_install.label: Jul. 2018 · Vista Alegre
items: Convoy to Amazonia. Floating platform co-built with residents of Puerto Miguel and Vista Alegre. Maintenance training. Integrated marker lighting.

loreto.label: End of 2018 · Loreto region
items: Official authorization to install in the waters of the Amazon River.

field_2019.label: 2019 · Follow-up mission
items: Testing under real conditions. Performance measurements, identification of new candidate villages.

suspendu.label: Since 2020 · On hold
items: Interrupted by COVID. Relaunch to be structured: data and open-source documentation available.

hydrama.caption_vista: Turbine and community in Vista Alegre

akuuvision.hero_subtitle: Discover the Amazon in a new way through an augmented reality app
akuuvision.what_para1: AKUUVision is an augmented reality app developed by AKUU to help people discover the Amazon differently. The rainforest often feels abstract to most people, so the association set out to build a concrete tool that everyone can use.
akuuvision.what_para2: The app combines field photos, residents' voices, and immersive views of the forest and river. At events, visitors scan physical materials to unlock content brought straight from Puerto Miguel: faces, voices, plants, water. A way to make real what is happening thousands of kilometres away.
akuuvision.what_para3: AKUUVision has been shown at many awareness events, including the Climax festival in Bordeaux and the Imaginarium Festival at UTC Compiègne. It was also presented on the Grenoble university campus through the IDEX Université Grenoble Alpes programme. In 2019, it won the national PIEED competition in Paris.
akuuvision.diffusion_1_text: Climax in Bordeaux, the Imaginarium Festival at UTC Compiègne, and other awareness events.
akuuvision.diffusion_2_text: Showcase on the Grenoble university campus as part of the IDEX Université Grenoble Alpes programme.
akuuvision.diffusion_3_text: In 2019, the project won the national PIEED competition in Paris.

cours_anglais.hero_subtitle: Giving residents English skills to take part in ecotourism on their own territory
cours_anglais.suspended_notice: This project is currently suspended since COVID due to lack of funding. Its relaunch is being planned.

marlon.bio: From a small Amazonian village, Marlon is the essential link between AKUU and local communities.

footer.slogan: In the Amazon and in France, AKUU connects volunteers and local communities around concrete solidarity projects.
